item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What the actual hell?

I can't fully comprehend what it was, but I've still enjoyed the read. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This sure is creative. Weird as hell but creative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the kind of weird that I like.

I wonder if there are multiple instances of the same smokable celebrity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Says who? Has this been brought up somewhere in an article or is it your headcanon? I'm pretty sure I've seen multiple humanoid SCPs or D-class tests done casually without any mention of the Committee at all.

EDIT: Typo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No Raising Arizona quote?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Before using instances of SCP-2060 in tests, a proposal must be submitted to the Ethics Committee for approval.

In any form of research using living subjects, ethics committees must sign off on them, making this statement as useful as saying "SCP-2060 are anomalous" - both of these statements are unspoken truths.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the coremost concept of sentient tobacco products, but then all the complications add up to an overall dislike. I don't like that they're mini humans, or celebrities, or that they occasionally plea for help, or that Nicholas Cage is in it, or the whole extra universe that's suddenly appearing, or the random strings of dialogue that I feel I was supposed to get a joke with but didn't. Too much stuff!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what the shit

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of the most wickedest, weirdest, upsetting and disturbing things I have ever read in this site and that deserves compliment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you had me at smokable Nicholas Cage. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love how weird it got by the time it was done. +1

Also I caught a typo, "browan rat" instead of "brown rat". Fixed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what do you mean nick cage is a former celeberty

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was a wonderful read from start to finish. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's alright. We understand. Most of us probably appreciate it greatly.

WE NEED A CHARLIE SHEEN ONE DAMN IT.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now this is how weird is done! Bravo!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the hell out of this, but the best part might be calling the celebrities "religious figures". I'm amazed at how well you continued to ramp up the weirdness as this goes.

But tell the truth, this was just an excuse to put a bunch of Nicolas Cage quotes into an article, wasn't it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You had me with the 15cm tall people, but lost me with the effect. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first it is temporary, then it starts to become permanent. Clarified that in the article, thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"This effect only lasts while an instance of SCP-2060 is in use, but is cumulative as more instances are used. In the event that instances with different political affiliations are used consecutively, the user will experience what appears to be a form of dissociative identity disorder, expressing solidarity with the political affiliations of all smoked instances simultaneously."

So, is it temporary, or not?

Also, why do the warning labels say the change is irreversible?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a little too silly for me, personally, but I won't vote because it's well-written and creative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find the DID connection a bit tasteless and off-the-mark, and I can't say I enjoy the idea of smokable celebrity figures anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's interesting, quirky, and really strange. I love the idea of someone sticking this little guy into their mouth and smoking him. The Nicholas Cage incident also really got me. Nice job on this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After several months of thought, I decided to replace the old SCP-2060. It was an amateurish article that was dragging down my average rating.
In its place is the new SCP-2060.

Many thanks to Cimmerian, SoundChaser, and thedeadlymoose for feedback.

See more of my work on my author page!

After almost three years I realized that I never cited the Nick Cage quotes I used. Hooray for hypocrisy!

I want to be Bob Denver on acid playing the accordion!

Originally acquired from this Independent article.

Killing me won't bring back your god damned honey!

From The Wicker Man (2006).

Put the bunny back in the box!

A classic from Con Air (1997).

Shoot him again, his soul's still dancing!

From Bad Lieutenant: Port of Call New Orleans (2009).
